T II-‾ 3(i)] MTNlSTRY OF CULTURE (Archaeological Survey oflntlia) NOTIFICATION New De lhi , the23rdAugust,2011 G.S.R. 63S(E).-Whereas draft National MonwnentsAuthority (Appoinbnent, Faoctions and Conduct ofBusiness) Rules, 20 I I were published as required by clause (a) ofsub-section (2) ofSection 31! of the Ancient Monuments and ArchaeologiCI11Sites and Remains Act, 1958 (24 of 1958) vide nwnber GS.R. 85(E) dated the I I th February, 2011 published in the Gazette oflndia, Extraordinary, Part U, Section 3, Sub-section (i) inviting objections and suggestions from all persons likely to be affected thereby, before the expiry ofthirty days from the date on which the copies of the Gazettenoti.fiCI1tion are made available to the pul>lic; 窶｢ And whereas, the said Gazette notification was made available to the public on J8'h February, 2011; And whereas, the objections received before the date so ﾂｷspecified have been duly considered and disposed by the Ceolral Government; Now, therefore, in exerci‾e of the powers conferred by clauses (ca). (cb), (cc), (cd), (ce), (cfJ, (cg) aod (cb) of the section 31! of the Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958 (24 of I958) the Central Government hereby makes the following rules , namely;- CHAPTER I PRELIMlNARY 1. Short title and commencement : - (1) These rules may be called th e National Momunents Authority (Conditions of Service of Chairman and Members of the Authority and Conduct of Business) Rules, 20 I I. (2) They shall come into force on their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Definitions :-In these ru les, unless the context otherwise requires, - , (a) ¥
means the Ancient Momunents and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958 (24 of 1958 ) ; (b) ¥
means the National Monuments Authority constit11ted under section ZO F ofthc Act; (c) ¥
means the Chairperson of the Authority, referred to in clause (a) of sub-section (2) of section 20F of the Act ; 9 -- 9 of 16 -- to THE GAZ!;Tfe OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PMT II-SEC. 3(i)J (d) ¥
means a whole-time member of the Authority, refetTed to in clause (b) of sub-section (2) of section 20F nfthe Act; (e) ¥
means a part-time member of the Authority, referred ¥¥to in clause (b) of sub-section (2) o( section 20 F of the Act; (f) ¥
means an activity, which concernsﾂｷ conStruction or reeonstruction t:3tima'ted to cost not less than rupees twenty crores. (g) ¥

means a municipal corporatiﾂｷon, mwlicipal committee, land development authorities cqnstituted under the respective State Acts, or special area development authority, village panchayat, zila parishad, hill development council, cantonment board or such other bodies, vested with the powers to control and regulate constructions and developmental activities in their respective areas; (h) ¥
means a Fom1 annexed to these ru les; (i) ¥

means a Schedule appended to these rules; (j) words and expressions defined in the Act, but not defined in these rules shall have the respective meanings assigned to them in the Act. CBAPTERll SALARIES, ALLOWANCES AND OTHER CONDITIONS OF SERVICE 3. Salaries, allowances and other conditions of se1-vice:- The- salary and allowances payable tO the whole-time Chairperson, whole-time membel'S and part-time members sjlall be as under.- (a) the wholeﾂｷtirne Cilairpcrson shafl be paid a fixed salary of rupees eighty thousand per month plus other allowances as are admissible to an ofl'icer of the rank of Secretary to the Government of India; (I?) the whole-time member sl1al l be paid salary in the Higher Administrative Grade in the scale of pay (Rs. 67000/- plus annual increment @3% 窶｢ 79000/-) plus su.ch other allowances as are admissible lo an officer of the rank of Additional Sec‾etary to the Government oflndia; Provided that if a whole-time member at Lhe lime of appointment is drawing a scale higher than the Higher Administrative Grade, his pay shall be protected as per the existing rules of the Government of India; (c) the part - time member shall be paid a sitting allowance of rupees three thousand for each day he attends U1e meeting of the Authority and shall be entitled to reimbursement of transportation charges at the rates as admissible to the whole-time member. 4. Service conditions of whole-time Chaifpcrson and members of the Authority :- (1) The Chairperson may resign from h is office in writing under his hand addressed to the Minister in charge of Culture but he shall continue to function as Chairperson until his resignation js accepted by lhe Minister. 窶｢ -- 10 of 16 -- 窶｢ ../¥

{'II'! II -‾ J(i)] (2) A whole-time member or part-time member may resign from his office in writing under his hand addressed to the Chairperson, but be shall Gontinue to function in the office as member until his resignation is accepted by the Chairperson. 5. General power and responsibility of Chairperson, Members an d Member- Secretary:- (I) The Chairperson shall have the powers of general superintendence and control in conduct of the affairs of the Authority, and be shall preside over the meetings ﾂｷ of the Authority . (2) TI1e whole-time members and part-time members shall have the responsibility to assist the Cl1airperson in arriving at decisions on various issues brought before the Authority. .(3) The Member Secretary shall be responsible for manpower planning, administration and all financial matters and perfonn the following functions, namely:- (i) all proposals submitted by the competent authorities to the Authority for consideration shall be processed by the Member Secretary in accordance with the heritage bye-laws; (ii) the Member-Secretary shall co-ordinate in preparation and issue of ouenda papers for the meetings of the Authority in consultation with the Chnirpcrsoo; (i ii j the Member-Secretary s ha ll be responsible for preparation of miuutcs uf the Authority meetings and issue of guidelines or interventions as the situation may arise in accordance witli the decisions of the Authority; and (iv) all recommendations of the Authority shall be authenticated hy the Member Secretary. CHAPTERJll PROCEDURE AND FORM FOR MAKING RECOMMENDATIONS 6. Grading, Categorisation an d classification of protect‾d monuments or protected aﾂｷrejls of national importanc e :- (I) For the purposes of making recommendation to the Central Government under sub-section (I) of section 4A, the Authority shall obtain public opinion and invite suggestions or objections from the public, within a period of 30 days for cu tegorisalion, grading and classification of moouments and archaeological sites declared to be of national importance having regard to the outstanding universal value, the historical, archaeological and architectural value and such other relevant factors; (2) The Authority shall after tak ing into consideration the objections and suggestions regarding categorisation, gr-clding anci classification of monuments and archaeological sites referred to in sub-rule {I), places such monuments and archaeological sites into the broad category of grading provided in the Schedule and shall accordingly make recommendations to the Central Government under sub-section (2) of Section 4A; {3) The Central Government sha ll on receipt of duly graded and classified lists of protected monuments and protected areas, uotify it in .the Official Gazcne and a lso eldlibit it on its Official web-site; II -- 11 of 16 -- 12 11IE GAZE'JTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY (PART ll‾ ‾:C. 3(i)J (4) The Authority shall obtain public opinion and invite suggestions or objections froni the public and hold consultations on each occasion when it proposes to extend tl1e prohibited or regulated area beyond one hundred ‾eters and two hundred meters respectively from u protected monument or protected area based on classification under section 4A , before sending its recommendations to the Central Government 7. Procedure by the National Monuments Authority:- The Authority shall monitor the working of the competent authority, particularly relating to - (i) receipt of applications by the competent authority from any person, who desires to carry out any construction, rc-eonstruction or repair or renovation; (ii) periodicity of disposal of applications with appropriate recommendations; (iii) the Member Secretary of the Aulllority while examining the applications ' received from lllc competent authority shall obtain requisite details, such as site inspection notes, ground conditions, heritage bye-laws and specific comments about tlle visual impact on regulated or prohibited area of the protected monument or protected area; (iv) obtain repor‾;s and comments on impact of major public works or projects und other constructions envisaged by the Central Government and the State Government, locul auiborities, municipal bodies, etc., private bodies, affecting the regulated or prohibited area indicated in the bye-laws around the protected monuments or archaeological sites and give its recommendations; (v) The Authority, in exceptional cases where lhe competent aulhority is unable to prepare the heritage by-laws within the specified period of sixty days, may extend the period hy another sixty days. 8. Agenda for the meeting of the Authority :- (I) The Authority shall deliberate on the following agenda, namely:- (i) examination and consideration of heritage bye-laws; (ii) examination and consideration of applications received for construction, re-construction, repairs and renovations from the competent authority; (ui) examination and consideration of major public projects, development projects and oilier projects related to public utility, etc.; (iv) examination of proposals regarding extending prohibited or regulated area, etc.; (v) examination of proposals regarding categorisation and classilication of protected monuments and protected area and obtaining publ.ic opinion and invite objections through notification, etc.; (vi) the Member Secretary shall include in the agenda for the meeting, any case which, in his opinion is urgent, irrespective of any deficiency in regarcl to particulars or delay in its receipt (2) The Authority shall evolve a systCJ:ll!Jf listing the above subject mntters received by it for consideration.- 9. Recommendation by Authority :- (I) The Member Secretary shall, within a period of forty-five days from the date of receipt of application from the competent authority, examine ll1e application, taking note of the heritage bye-Jaws and have it included in the meeting of the Authority; -- 12 of 16 -- { ¥
